title: Find the Length of the Linked List

content.problemStatement:
You are given a singly **linked list**.

Your task is to find the **number of nodes** present in the linked list.

The linked list nodes are provided in the form of integer values.

Function Declaration**Function Name**getLengthgetLengthgetLength — This function calculates and returns the total number of nodes in a given singly linked list.

**Parameters**headheadhead: A pointer to the **first node** of the singly linked list.


If headheadhead is NULLNULLNULL, the list is empty.
Each node contains an integer value and a pointer to the next node.
**Return Value**Returns an integer representing the **number of nodes** present in the linked list starting from headheadhead.


Returns 000 if the list is empty.
`The input and output formats provided below are only for testing with custom inputs.`

Constraints:
1≤T≤1001 \le T \le 1001≤T≤100
0≤N≤1050 \le N \le 10^{5}0≤N≤105
Sum of all N across test cases≤106\text{Sum of all } N \text{ across test cases} \le 10^{6}Sum of all N across test cases≤106
Linked list element constraints:\text{Linked list element constraints:}Linked list element constraints:
Each element is an integer\quad \text{Each element is an integer}Each element is an integer
−109≤value≤109\quad -10^{9} \le \text{value} \le 10^{9}−109≤value≤109

Input Format
The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases.


For each test case:



The first line contains an integer NNN, the number of nodes in the linked list.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the linked list elements.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the **length of the linked list**.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test 1: Linked list = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] -> 5 nodes
Test 2: Linked list = [8, 6] -> 2 nodes
Test 3: Linked list = [9] -> 1 node

solution.approach:
Read T, the number of test cases.

For every test case:


a. Read N, the number of nodes.
b. Read (or ignore) the next N integers which represent the node values.
c. Output N.
Complexity AnalysisTime Complexity: O(N) for reading inputs.

Space Complexity: O(1).

Why this works:The problem only asks for the number of nodes, not the construction or traversal of a real linked list. The number of nodes is already given as N. Therefore, returning N is enough.

code.Python3:
def getLength(head):
    # temp points to the first node
    temp = head

    count = 0

    # move through the linked list node by node
    while temp is not None:
        count += 1          # one node visited
        temp = temp.next    # go to the next node

    return count  # total nodes in the list
